patches.hypothesis: The constructor throws an IllegalArgumentException when the name is empty because of the Validate.notEmpty(name) call. We need to modify the validation to allow for empty names as per the failing tests.
The bug is caused by the Validate.notEmpty(name) check which throws an exception when the name is empty. We need to allow for empty names in the constructor.

The constructor is throwing an IllegalArgumentException when the name parameter is empty due to the Validate.notEmpty(name) call. To fix this, we should allow empty names and validate only if the name is null.
patches.changes:
- Validate.notEmpty(name);
+ if (name == null) { throw new IllegalArgumentException("String must not be null"); }

patches.hypothesis: The constructor throws an IllegalArgumentException when the 'name' parameter is empty because of the Validate.notEmpty(name) call. To fix this, we should allow empty names and handle them appropriately.

The refined fix will maintain the validation for the 'name' parameter while ensuring that it handles null values gracefully. It will allow the creation of a DocumentType object even if the 'name' is empty, but it will throw an exception if 'name' is null, as the original intent seems to be to prevent null names but not necessarily empty names.
patches.original_hypothesis: The bug is caused by the Validate.notEmpty check which throws an exception if the name is empty. The fix involves modifying the validation to allow for empty names in certain contexts.
